organizations.role: Public supporter / donor (self-stated pro-Israel political interest)
organizations.notes: Loewy Law Firm leadership bio and Texas Hillel alumni spotlight both state AIPAC is among organizations the Loewys actively support. Adam has "keen interest in pro-Israel politics and is involved with AIPAC." Disambiguation: Velasquez batch Loewy, Adam / law loewy / Austin 78731 = Adam Jacob Loewy, Loewy Law Firm P.C.
Loewy Law Firm bio lists AIPAC among organizations Phil and Adam actively support.

organizations.org: Shalom Austin (Jewish Federation of Greater Austin)
organizations.role: 2022 Annual Campaign Co-Chair; Joe Krassner Campaign Leadership Award recipient
organizations.notes: Shalom Austin CEO letter and Loewy Law Firm bio confirm Adam and brother Phil served as 2022 Campaign Co-Chairs. Adam received the Joe Krassner Campaign Leadership Award from Jewish Federation of Austin.

organizations.org: Jewish Federations of North America - Prime Ministers Council
organizations.notes: Adam and Phil Loewy joined 150 members of Jewish Federations Prime Ministers Council (national top-giving society).

organizations.org: Shalom Austin (Jewish Federation of Greater Austin)
organizations.role: Board Chair; Executive Committee; Philanthropy Committee Chair
organizations.notes: Phil (Philippa) Loewy of Loewy Law Firm serves on the Shalom Austin Board and Executive Committee as Philanthropy Committee Chair, and is confirmed as Board Chair. Disambiguation: Velasquez donor Loewy, Phil / law firm loewy / Austin 78731 matches firm address and family.

organizations.org: Jewish Federations of North America - Prime Ministers Council
organizations.notes: Phil and Adam Loewy joined Jewish Federations Prime Ministers Council.

organizations.role: True Colors: Shake the Hate Featured Speaker (2012)
organizations.notes: Featured speaker. Muslim woman who shared story about facing discrimination post-9/11 in Austin. Introduced by ADL Austin Community Director Karen Gross.

organizations.role: 2010 Torch of Liberty Co-Chair; 2022 Maislin Humanitarian Award honoree
organizations.notes: With husband Steven Tomlinson

Amber K. Goodwin is a lawyer and gun-violence-prevention advocate now an Assistant District Attorney in Travis County (matches 'Lawyer'/'Travis County', Austin 78701). She founded/led the Community Justice Action Fund (Black- and Brown-led gun violence prevention org) and was first National Advocacy Director at Giffords. She is herself a 2026 Austin City Council District 1 candidate (endorsed by GIFFORDS PAC).

She is herself a 2026 Austin City Council District 1 candidate (endorsed by GIFFORDS PAC).

organizations.org: Greater Austin Hispanic Chamber of Commerce / Greater Austin Chamber of Commerce
organizations.role: Former Board Chair (GAHCC); former Executive Board member (Greater Austin Chamber)
organizations.notes: Paul M. Saldana, president/principal of Saldana Public Relations in Austin; former Austin ISD Trustee for District 6 (2015-2017) and Board Vice-President (2016-17), and former chief of staff to Austin Mayor Gus Garcia. Occupation ('PR Consultant'), employer string ('Saldana PR') and south-Travis-County location (Manchaca 78652) all corroborate. Confirmed NOT a Texas Ethics Commission registered lobbyist (the only Saldana in TEC lobby records is Amanda Saldana of Edinburg) and no match in the City of Austin City Clerk registered-lobbyist list.

Gregory Keith Wooldridge, 65 (b. 1961), West Lake Hills TX, co-owns 501 Rocky River Road, West Lake Hills 78746 with his wife Lynne Dobson — matching the filing's 78746 / West Lake Hills / 'Retired, Self'. He is a retired newspaperman (Austin American-Statesman sports and features editor) who with Dobson founded the Tejemos Foundation, a major Austin philanthropic funder. NOT the Blue Angels commander: that Greg 'Boss' Wooldridge is a retired Navy captain, roughly 78, based in Oregon, where he ran for governor in 2018 — different age, state, and career.

organizations.org: Greater Austin Asian Chamber of Commerce (GAACC)
organizations.notes: Founder, President & CEO of SOAL Technologies, an Austin-based IT/government-staffing firm at 8870 Business Park Dr Suite 200, Austin TX 78759 (matches donor zip 78759); nominated for Austin Business Journal 'Best CEO'. Also serves as a City of Austin Asian American Quality of Life Advisory Commission Commissioner and as past Chairman/Board Member of the Greater Austin Asian Chamber of Commerce (GAACC).
Major Austin tech accelerator

Jessica V. Huynh is an Austin criminal-defense trial attorney who runs JVH Law, PLLC (self-employed, matching the filed employer "Self" + occupation "Attorney"); she spent 11 years as a prosecutor in Galveston, Williamson and Travis counties, was lead trainer at the Travis County DA's Office, and is an adjunct at UT Law. Zip 78737 (southwest Austin on the Travis/Hays line) is corroborated by her listed membership in the Hays County Bar Association alongside Austin-area bar groups. She was the 2022 Democratic primary candidate for Travis County's 331st District Court, consistent with the pipeline's FEC match showing 9 federal contributions at a fully Democratic partisan lean. FEC and TEC searches surfaced no federal ideological-PAC giving and no Texas lobbyist registration.

organizations.org: AFSCME Local 1624 (Austin/Travis County public employees union)
organizations.role: Past President (2023-2024); 2025 candidate for President; current member
organizations.notes: Benjamin Cornelio Suddaby is an Operations Specialist II in Travis County Purchasing (county staff directory lists benjamin.suddaby@traviscountytx.gov; public payroll records show the same title), matching the filing's occupation "Operations Specialist" and employer "Travis County". He is also a longtime AFSCME Local 1624 activist who served as Local 1624 president (2023-2024) and is a Local 1624 delegate to the Austin Central Labor Council; the heavy Democratic federal giving pattern (178 contributions) is consistent with a union officer. No TEC lobbyist registration and no ideological-PAC contributions surfaced.

organizations.org: Office of Austin Mayor Steve Adler
organizations.notes: Stephanie Kay Trinh is an Austin attorney (Texas bar #24089260, licensed 2014, UT Law 2014) who served as Chief of Staff to U.S. Rep. Greg Casar from January 2023 until March 2026, and earlier as senior advisor to Austin Mayor Steve Adler, policy advisor/counsel to then-Council Member Casar, and a fellow and staff attorney at Texas RioGrande Legal Aid. The filed employer 'Self' is consistent with her March 2026 departure from congressional staff, and the Austin location, attorney occupation, and heavily Democratic federal giving pattern all corroborate identity. Best match is Gary Allan Cobb, longtime Travis County assistant district attorney (24 years, director of the Grand Jury/Intake Division), UT Law 1986, who ran in the 2008 and 2016 Democratic primaries for Travis County DA and said after losing to Margaret Moore in 2016 that he would not stay at the office. Zip 78749 is corroborated by contemporaneous reporting describing him as vice president of the Southwest Austin Democrats (78749 is southwest Austin) and by a City of Austin filing listing a 512-899 (southwest Austin) home number. 'Retired' occupation and a heavily Democratic 92-donation federal giving history (FEC lean 0.90) are consistent with his documented Democratic-primary political activity. Confidence held at medium because no public record directly ties the 78749 address to this specific Gary Cobb, and there are other Austin-area Gary Cobbs (including one whose obituary appears in 2019). Full bio read of the Austin Monitor profile and Martindale/State Bar listings found no lobbying, energy, firearms, defense, or Israel/Palestine-related roles on any side; no TEC lobbyist registration found.

organizations.notes: Terrysa Guerra is Co-Executive Director of United for Respect (and its Education Fund), matching the donor's employer string exactly; the first name is distinctive and her biography is Texas-rooted (raised in Los Fresnos, TX; Texas A&M graduate). Before joining United for Respect in 2018 she worked for the national and Texas state Democratic parties, managed campaigns for two Texas state senators and a state representative, and was deputy campaign manager for Wendy Davis's gubernatorial bid. At United for Respect she built the organization's policy and political programs — retail-worker organizing, guaranteed severance pay, scheduling standards, and the Toys R Us worker hardship fund. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found under her name. FEC search run: the pipeline records 20 federal donations; no ideological PAC ties on any side of the Israel/Palestine, firearms, energy, or defense spectrums surfaced, and committee-level detail was not retrievable (FEC browser is a JS app; open FEC API returned HTTP 429).

Huynh is an Austin criminal-defense trial attorney who runs JVH Law, PLLC (self-employed, matching the filed employer "Self" + occupation "Attorney"); she spent 11 years as a prosecutor in Galveston, Williamson and Travis counties, was lead trainer at the Travis County DA's Office, and is an adjunct at UT Law. Zip 78737 (southwest Austin on the Travis/Hays line) is corroborated by her listed membership in the Hays County Bar Association alongside Austin-area bar groups. She was the 2022 Democratic primary candidate for Travis County's 331st District Court, consistent with the pipeline's FEC match showing 9 federal contributions at a fully Democratic partisan lean. organizations.org: Texas House Subcommittee on Juvenile Justice (public testimony)
